---
title: "Giorgio Maarraoui"
---

::: {.hero-panel}
::: {.hero-grid}
::: {.hero-copy}
![](img/portrait.jpg){width=190 .portrait}

## Economist | Market Research | Data Analytics
PhD in Economics, University of Manchester

I am an economist and quantitative researcher with experience spanning **causal inference**, **policy evaluation**, **market and welfare analysis**, and **applied data analytics**. My work combines rigorous econometric design with large, messy real-world datasets to answer decision-relevant questions clearly and defensibly.

Alongside my PhD, I work with the **World Bank Poverty & Equity Global Practice**, where I contribute to **Commitment to Equity (CEQ)** assessments and broader fiscal-incidence analysis. Across academic and applied settings, I build evidence on how shocks, incentives, and institutions shape behaviour, distributional outcomes, and policy performance.
:::

::: {.hero-aside}
### Current Roles

**University of Manchester**  
PhD Economist and Quantitative Researcher

**World Bank**  
Economic Consultant, Poverty & Equity Global Practice

### Research Areas

::: {.flat-list}
- Causal inference and quasi-experimental design
- Market research and behavioural analysis
- Data analytics, segmentation, and forecasting
- Policy evaluation, welfare, and distributional analysis
:::

### Links

::: {.flat-list}
- [CV](cv/Giorgio_Maarraoui_CV.pdf)
- [Google Scholar](https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=YmJMv2wAAAAJ&hl=en)
- [ORCID](https://orcid.org/0000-0002-2749-5782)
- [Teaching](teaching.qmd)
:::
:::
:::

::: {.feature-card}
### Analytical Profile

My background is grounded in the type of analytical work valued by research, strategy, and economics teams in industry: defining hard questions precisely, building credible identification strategies, linking multiple data sources, and translating results into clear implications. I work comfortably across **econometrics**, **causal design**, **survey and observational data**, **high-dimensional spatial datasets**, and **decision-focused communication**.
:::

::: {.feature-card}
### Ongoing Applied Work

At the World Bank, I work on fiscal-incidence and equity analysis using the CEQ framework. Current projects include **forthcoming 2025 CEQ studies for Pakistan and Djibouti**, alongside earlier work on poverty, inequality, and gender in Djibouti. The work involves large administrative and household datasets, distributional modelling, policy counterfactuals, and concise communication for decision-makers.
:::

::: {.feature-card}
### Teaching and Course Design

I teach across microeconomics and econometrics at the University of Manchester, including **Intermediate Microeconomics**, **Econometrics**, and **Applied Microeconometrics (MSc)**. I have also **lectured and redesigned the MSc Microeconometrics course**, strengthening its empirical focus, structure, and delivery. That experience sharpened my ability to explain technical ideas clearly, structure quantitative material, and make empirical evidence usable for different audiences.
:::
